title: Boids Flocking Simulator
description: A 2D implementation of Craig Reynolds' flocking algorithm featuring separation/alignment/cohesion steering behaviors, toroidal wrapping, predator evasion, click-to-spawn, and real-time telemetry.
author: Girish Madarkar
features: Craig Reynolds' three steering behaviors: separation (proximity-weighted repel vector), alignment (neighbor average heading), cohesion (center-of-mass attraction), each with independent weight sliders
Neighbor perception: O(n²) distance-squared comparison within configurable radius (30-150px), using toroidal shortest-path distance for correct edge wrapping
Toroidal space: seamless edge wrapping — coordinates teleport to opposite boundary on exit, distance calculation uses min(dx, W-dx) for correct wrap-around proximity
Predator agent: red diamond-shaped boid that chases nearby boids; standard boids apply enhanced separation against predator, causing scattering evasion behavior
Click-to-spawn: canvas click/touch spawns 10 boids at cursor coordinates with randomized velocity vectors
Triangle rendering: per-boid isosceles triangle drawn via ctx.rotate(Math.atan2(vy, vx)) with size proportional to speed (7 + speed×2), neon cyan glow
Cluster estimation: BFS flood-fill grouping boids within 2× perception radius, displayed as cluster tally in telemetry
Telemetry panel: active agent count, mean group velocity (average speed magnitude), cluster count, updated per frame
Speed limiting: max speed slider (1-6) enforced via per-frame clamping of velocity magnitude
Resize handler: canvas buffer re-mapped on window resize, boid positions remain valid via toroidal clamping
